goals: run a trading competition this fall

cards.idea: Model Crestview Trading Club after Traders at Berkeley, which hosts an annual intercollegiate trading competition and teaches quantitative finance skills to undergraduates.
cards.whyItFitsYou: Traders at Berkeley demonstrates how a student-run trading organization can build a premier competition, attract industry sponsorships, and grow membership through education and competition—directly aligning with Crestview's goals to run a fall trading competition and land sponsors.

evidence.claim: Traders at Berkeley hosts the annual Berkeley Trading Competition, teaches the Introduction to Quantitative Finance course, and connects students with the industry's leading trading and technology firms.

comparables.name: Traders at Berkeley
comparables.whyComparable: Peer student-run trading club that hosts an annual intercollegiate competition, offers quantitative finance education, and engages industry partners.
notablePlays: Teaches Introduction to Quantitative Finance DeCal course open to all students
Connects students with leading trading and technology firms as sponsors

executionSteps: Study Traders at Berkeley's competition format (2-day event with trading and quantitative research challenges) and course curriculum to adapt for Crestview's fall timeline.
Identify potential sponsors from the trading and fintech firms that partner with Berkeley, and use their model as a case study in sponsorship outreach.
Launch a basic quantitative finance course or workshop series alongside the competition to build membership and demonstrate educational value to prospective sponsors.

cards.idea: Register Crestview students for the Stevens High Frequency Trading Competition (HFTC-26) and use it as a recruitment and sponsorship platform.
cards.whyItFitsYou: HFTC-26 is open to all qualified undergraduates and graduate students worldwide, offering Crestview a prestigious competition to showcase members' skills while attracting sponsors impressed by student participation in a rigorous, industry-recognized event.

evidence.claim: The School of Business at Stevens Institute of Technology is pleased to announce the annual Stevens High Frequency Trading Competition (HFTC-26)! This rigorous, high-stakes event is open to all qualified undergraduate and graduate students enrolled in an accredited university anywhere in the world.
This rigorous, high-stakes event is open to all qualified undergraduate and graduate students enrolled in an accredited university anywhere in the world.
evidence.sourceName: High Frequency Trading Competition 2026 - Stevens Institute of Technology

executionSteps: Form a Crestview team and submit entries to HFTC-26 before the registration deadline.
Highlight team participation in sponsorship pitches to demonstrate engagement with prestigious industry competitions.
Use the competition as a membership recruitment tool by showcasing the rigor and prestige of the event to prospective traders.

cards.idea: Virtual and simulated trading competitions are becoming standard practice in student finance organizations, combining competitive gameplay with guest speaker sessions and multi-format learning.
cards.whyItFitsYou: Multiple prominent competitions (Arbiter Stock Trading Club, Bloomberg Global Trading Challenge, Stevens competitions) integrate simulated markets with educational programming, validating Crestview's hybrid approach of a fall competition plus speaker series for member engagement and sponsorship appeal.

evidence.claim: Students will compete with simulated trading and rewards. In addition there will be guest speakers and interaction to learn trading from different perspectives.

Teams of 3-5 students will compete against other top students around the world. The winning team will have the highest Time Weighted Relative Return relative to the Bloomberg World Large, Mid & Small Cap Price Return Index (WLS Index). The competition gives students hands-on experience of real-world investing.
evidence.sourceName: Stock Trading Competitions - Naveen Jindal School of Management

evidence.claim: Over the two day competition, participants will compete in a series of trading and quantitative research challenges developed by Traders at Berkeley.
evidence.sourceName: Berkeley Trading Competition - Traders at Berkeley

executionSteps: Design Crestview's fall competition to include both simulated market challenges and guest speaker sessions featuring industry professionals.
Emphasize to sponsors that the multi-format approach (competition + education) aligns with industry-standard practices and broadens member engagement.
Promote the speaker series as a draw for members who may not compete but want to learn trading fundamentals.

queries: trading competition student orgs fall 2026 strategies
